content:
I have created a harness that evaluates a C block device driver against its rust counterpart (using the null_blk/rnull pair as the main samples) over a variety of metrics considering static analysis, performance, fuzzing and contribution history.

It uses statistics to help gather evidence over if a driver should be migrated to rust or left as is.

If some one wants to give it a look and provide some feedback. Let me know and I’ll fill you in with the details.
I am curious of your findings. When I sent the first rust null block series a few years back, I did a (manual) analysis of the commit log to see how many commits was related to memory safety issues.
Awesome. I’ll send the project via e-mail
